name: tdd-implementation
summary: Red-green-refactor discipline: write a failing test before writing the production code, then refactor with tests green.
when_to_use: New behavior with a clear specification. Skip when specification is emergent or when reproducing an existing bug (use fix-bug-from-trace instead).

phases.name: Red
steps: Identify one atomic unit of new behavior (one test case).
Write the test using the project's existing test patterns (check jest/vitest/pytest config).
Run the test and confirm it fails for the expected reason (not a compile error).
Commit the failing test as a separate commit with message 'test: add failing test for {behavior}'.

phases.name: Green
steps: Implement the minimum code required to make the test pass.
Resist the urge to generalize — that's the next phase.
Run the full test suite; no other tests may regress.

phases.name: Refactor
steps: Improve the code structure: naming, extraction, dedup.
No new behavior — tests still pass without changes.
Commit with message 'refactor: {what-was-improved}' if refactor was substantive.

exit_criteria: All target behaviors covered; test suite green; coverage metrics meet the project's threshold.
anti_patterns: Writing multiple tests at once — breaks the red-green signal.
Generalizing in the Green phase — hides design pressure.
Skipping Refactor because tests are green — accrues debt.
